level_signals: 10+ years of experience in program management, communications operations, or closely-related discipline
Comp range $265K-$295K base, consistent with Senior IC at Anthropic
Expected to influence senior stakeholders and partner closely with comms leadership

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Have 10+ years of experience in program management, communications operations, or a closely-related discipline, including significant time working in or alongside communications teams
Deeply understand how communications functions work — how campaigns are built, how media moments unfold, and how crisis response operates in practice
Have led complex, high-visibility programs under pressure, with a track record of bringing calm, structure, and momentum to chaotic situations
Have experience with high-growth teams whose processes need to scale as fast as they are
Can influence and align senior stakeholders across very different functions and working styles, without relying on formal authority
Possess impeccable organizational skills, paired with the judgment to know when process creates clarity and when it gets in the way
Think strategically in real time — connecting the immediate fire drill to the bigger picture and helping leadership see around corners
Are genuinely flexible: comfortable with last-minute pivots, shifting priorities, and plans that change mid-flight
Are relentless about follow-through: you don't let things drop, even when the team is at its busiest
Communicate with exceptional clarity, especially when synthesizing messy situations for executive audiences
Care about ensuring that transformative AI systems are developed safely
Minimum education: Bachelor's degree or an equivalent combination of education, training, and/or experience

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: In-house communications or agency experience at a high-growth technology company
Direct experience supporting crisis communications or rapid-response efforts
Experience standing up program management or operations functions from scratch
Familiarity with the AI landscape and the issues shaping the public conversation around it

scope.surface: Communications team operations — major product launches, announcements, reactive/crisis situations, external, internal, and editorial communications
scope.business_model: internal function support at an AI safety company
primary_metrics_inferred: Speed and quality of crisis response
core_responsibilities: Run the operating rhythm for major campaigns: planning, prioritization, status reporting, and retrospectives
Translate fast-changing, ambiguous projects into clear workstreams, owners, timelines, and decision points
Serve as a strategic thought partner: bring recommendations, surface risks and tradeoffs early, and drive decisions under pressure
Build alignment and drive accountability across cross-functional partners without formal authority
Anticipate operational bottlenecks before they hit and resolve them with incomplete information and compressed timelines
Design and continuously improve the team's operating framework: launching playbooks, crisis response protocols, escalation paths, and cross-team coordination models

green_flags: Compensation range disclosed and competitive ($265K-$295K base)
Clear description of the role's purpose and day-to-day operational context
Explicitly encourages applications from underrepresented groups and those who don't meet every qualification
Senior, high-visibility role with direct partnership with comms leadership and executive teams
red_flags: Hybrid policy requires at least 25% office time in San Francisco — eliminates fully-remote candidates
10+ years minimum experience is a high bar for a non-people-management IC role
Role is highly context-dependent (communications fluency required); hard to enter from a purely operational background without comms exposure

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from 'senior role' language + comp range + years requirement)
seniority.manages_people (inferred as false; no mention of direct reports)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (inferred from responsibilities; not explicit in JD)
location.cities_listed (San Francisco inferred from HQ mention; no explicit city listed for this role)
ambiguities: The JD says 'some roles may require more time in our offices' — it is unclear if this role specifically requires more than 25%; treated as 25% minimum here.

The 'Required field of study' is stated as 'A field relevant to the role as demonstrated through coursework, training, or professional experience' — effectively open-ended; no specific degree field required.
